observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Provide additional manpower to augment the Inventory Committee in performing their duties and functions and require the head of various offices to oversee the conduct of physical count of existing PPEs found in their respective offices and prepare the report to be submitted and verified by the Inventory Committee;
Direct the Municipal Treasurer/Property Custodian to update the RPCPPE using the data provided by various offices to reflect the actual count and value of the PPE items maintained by the Municipality and reconcile the same with the accounting records; and
Conduct the regular physical count of all its PPE on a semestral basis, as required by the Manual on NGAs.

We recommended that Management:
Conduct a complete physical count of inventory and PPE and prepare the report (RPCI and RPCPPE) thereon;
Prepare a list of reclassified PPE items whether issued or in the custody of Property Division/Unit, recompute the carrying amount of PPE items, and prepare a breakdown for items to be classified as an expense and for items to be classified as semi-expendable property; and
Revisit the adjustment made and effect an adjusting entry to correct the related asset and equity account thereafter.
We recommended that the Management: a) instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to locate the documents and details of the recorded time deposit
observations.managementAction: and b) remit and/or reconcile the Cash – Local Treasury on a daily basis.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:
Stop the grant of additional cash advances to Accountable Officers with unliquidated cash advances until such time that the previous cash advances are fully settled;
Require the concerned officers and employees to immediately settle/liquidate their outstanding cash advances through submission of complete liquidation reports, otherwise, enforce the withholding of salary in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002; and
Monitor and adopt adequate control measures to effect the immediate  and timely submission of liquidation reports  to avoid accumulation and ballooning of receivable balances.

We recommended that Management require the Collecting Officer to ensure that collection of entrance fees are remitted daily and deposited promptly and intact in accordance with Section 32 of the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
We recommended that Management furnish a copy of the contract of the lease to COA if already executed between LGU and FBSP, or expedite the execution thereof if none is executed yet.

We recommended that the Management strengthen its campaign and widen the scope of its information drive to generate more demand for the vaccine and continue its vaccination rollout until the desired local herd immunity is attained.

We recommended that the Management:
Instruct the MHO to render a monthly report to the Accounting Office on the receipt, issuance, and balances of vaccines and other inventories directly received from outside sources; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain PPE Ledger Cards and Registry of Donated Relief Goods – DRRM, reflect the full cost of ending inventory of goods/vaccines received and issued, and record in the books the cost of donated PPE less its total accumulated depreciation.

We recommended that the Management:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to regularly conduct the reconciliation of the Due to NGAs account against relevant records, adjust all the reconciling items noted for the fair presentation of the Financial Statements, and exert diligent effort in locating the supporting documents  to fast track the reconciliation;
Immediately liquidate unexpended/excess balance of the fund transfers upon completion of the projects; and
Direct the concerned Department Heads to coordinate among themselves for the keeping of essential records such as a Memorandum of Agreement or any applicable guidelines.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents noted and strictly abide by the rules, laws, and regulations as discussed in PD No. 1445 regarding the submission of complete supporting documents.

We recommended that the Management:
Revise the pro-forma contract maintained by the Human Resource Management Office (HRMO) by specifying the work assignments, duties and responsibilities, and technical expertise to be accomplished to facilitate comparison and evaluation of actual work performed;
Submit the written request from the Head of Office and authority from LCE  concerning their claims for overtime services; and
Ensure compliance with Sec 4(6) of PD No. 1445 that claims against government funds shall be supported with complete supporting documents.

We recommended the Management to:
Through the accountant to verify and reconcile the outstanding withholding taxes  to determine the correct amount for remittance, and prepare adjusting entries as necessary; and
Ensure in all instances that taxes withheld are remitted intact and within the period prescribed by pertinent Revenue Regulations.

We recommended that the Management:
Instruct  the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to revisit the PCW-DM-DILG-NEDA JMC and ensure compliance thereof regarding their roles and responsibilities in the gender mainstreaming and policy formulation of the Municipality;
Require the GFPS and GAD Focal Person to comply with the provisions of the aforementioned circular on the preparation and submission of GAD Accomplishment Report (AR) and submit a GAD AR reflecting the actual cost or expenditures incurred; and
Instruct the GAD Focal Person to maintain a monitoring of all the costs associated with the implementation of GAD PPAs to serve as a basis for the preparation of the GAD AR and establish a GAD database that will include gender statistics and sex-disaggregated data to serve as inputs for planning, budgeting, programming, and policy formulation.

We recommended that Management require that the:

Municipal Accountant (MA) and Municipal Treasurer (MT) to exert best efforts to locate the details of the time deposit and identify the employee/official accountable for this and require the MA to book up the said accountability. Also, 

Management investigates, take action and impose sanctions, if warranted and when needed, to avoid the same to happen again.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
execute all the projects within the time frame programmed for their completion to effectively and timely extend to the constituents the benefits of the agency’s projects; and
adequately monitor the progress of projects including regular updates on cause of delay in implementation.

We recommended that Management:
adhere to the step by step procurement process provided by the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; and
ensure consistency of information in the bidding procurement documents prepared and submitted.

We recommended that Management:
strictly adhere to the provision of Section 7 of Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of Republic Act (RA) No. 9184 which provides that all procurement of the LGU shall be within the approved budget of the Procuring Entity (PE) and should be meticulously and judiciously planned; and
ensure that the ABC is always aligned with the approved Budget and Annual Procurement Plan.

We recommended that the Management:
observe prudence in expending the Municipality’s budget particularly in purchasing high end vehicle in times of calamity; and
focus more on the projects/programs/activities that directly address the current situations/problems of the LGU.

We recommended the Management to:
review the accomplishment reports submitted by the MHO personnel and recompute the COVID-19 SRA to the eligible MHO personnel; and
refund the amount of ₱88,636.35 from those employees who were not eligible to claim SRA.

We recommended that Management:
strictly adhere to the provision of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 and utilize the fund that support disaster risk management activities such as, disaster prevention and mitigation, disaster preparedness, disaster response and disaster rehabilitation and recovery; and
submit Distribution List for the 8,550 kilos (171 sacks) of rice distributed to each recipient with complete details as to name, address, number of kilos, purpose and date given.

We recommended that Management:
review the granting of excessive communication allowances and consider the application of postpaid and prepaid promos or packages offered by the available cellular network and internet providers with high connectivity in the Municipality; and
Draft and issue a new SB resolution prescribing clear guidelines and policies on the grant, utilization, control and monitoring of communication allowance.
We recommended that Management immediately return any unused/unexpended balances to the National Treasury or revert it to Unappropriated Surplus in the General Fund, if any.

We recommended that the Management:
Disburse and charge expenditures to GAD Budget if supported with the prescribed guidelines, policies and LGU GAD Code;
Require the Municipal Accountant to verify the completeness of attached supporting documents; and
Require the GAD focal person to submit the Annual Gender and Development Accomplishment Report for 2021.

We recommended that the Management: (a) fast track the reconciliation of the unremitted taxes withheld and ensure that monthly taxes withheld were remitted intact and timely; (b) require the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to remit immediately the unremitted tax withheld; and (c) impose penalties for late remittances of tax withheld against the employee/s that caused the delay.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the:
Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Municipal Assessor to come up with a correct Statement of Real Property Tax Receivables starting from CY 2019 and onward;
Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of RPT/SET Receivables; and
Municipal Treasurer to take extra effort in collecting RPT/SET from delinquent taxpayers; update the RPT Tax system or evaluate the effectiveness and efficiency of the RPT Tax and consider replacing or upgrading the system; and verify the correctness of the SRPTR before  furnishing the same to the Accounting and Auditor’s Office.

We recommended that Management require the:
Municipal Treasurer to: 1) prepare a Report on Local Road Network; 2) maintain local road inventory and road map; and 3) maintain property cards for all roads and its components;
Municipal Accountant to: 1) maintain subsidiary ledger cards  for roads and road components for every road network; 2) prepare lapsing schedule showing the computation of the depreciation of each component of the Road Network System account; 3) disclose the total Road Network System account in the Notes to the Financial Statements; and 4) make the necessary adjusting entry on repair and maintenance recorded to Road Network System account.
OIC-Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to fast track the reconciliation of the unremitted taxes withheld and settle immediately the outstanding trust liabilities with the BIR totaling ₱779,775.01 and henceforth comply with the prescribed period of remittance in accordance with Revenue Regulations No. 2-98.
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to properly reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts.

We recommended that Management require the submission of the following documents prior to approval of hazard pay:
a) approved list of position in its plantilla with the corresponding job description/actual duties that describes the basis for justifying such positions who are exposed to occupational risks and assigned to places as stated in Section 16.1.1of the IRR of RA No. 9433; and
b)  detailed daily accomplishment report of the MSWDO showing proof of actual exposure to hazardous situations to justify the payment of hazard allowance.

We recommended that Management: a) require the supplier to change the delivered 2 units LED monitor (43”and 32”) with 50” monitor being the requirement of the LGU as stated in the Purchase Order, else the amount of ₱87,284 pertaining to the cost of delivered monitors shall be disallowed in audit; b) require the concerned LGU official/employee to refund the amount of ₱950.00 pertaining to undelivered items pending issuance of Notice of Disallowance; c) instruct the BAC to be cautious in the review/verification of the submitted documents by prospective suppliers/bidders; and d) ensure that in the preparation of Purchase Request/s, deliverables including its specifications are properly indicated.

To reiterate our previous years’ audit recommendations,  we recommended that Management: a) stop the practice of resorting to cash advance in the procurement of goods and services required during the conduct of the annual activities/events of the Municipality; and b) require the refund of the amount of ₱26,256.81 representing taxes that should have been withheld for the procurement of goods and services made through cash advances and remit the same to the BIR pending issuance of Notice of Disallowance.

We recommended that Management relieve the Internal Auditor of all other positions and designations incompatible with her position as Internal Auditor and for the Internal Auditor to start doing her mandated tasks.
We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer the immediate submission of the 36 DVs with its corresponding supporting documents pending issuance of Notice of Suspension.

We recommended and Management agreed to require: a) the Municipal Development Council to judiciously plan the programs/ projects/ activities to be funded out of the 20% EDF in accordance with the mandate of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 and cause the full implementation of  projects during the year of appropriation in order to achieve the purpose for which they were established; and b) require the Office of the Municipal Budget Officer to diligently furnish our office with MDC Resolutions and the equivalent Sanggunian Bayan Ordinance which alters or makes changes in the EDF appropriation for easy monitoring of EDF projects.

We recommended that Management require the: Municipal Accountant to conduct reconciliation of all bank accounts and submit bank reconciliation statements within 10 days from receipt of bank statements; Municipal Accountant to maintain Subsidiary Ledgers for Cash-in-Bank-LCCA account; Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to conduct reconciliation of cashbook and accounting record balances on a regular basis; and Municipal Treasurer to maintain a separate cashbook for each bank account.

We reiterated our prior years’ audit recommendations that Management:  a.) require the concerned municipal officials and employees and Job Order Workers who are still in the service to settle immediately their outstanding cash advances, else deduct from their salaries the unliquidated cash advances; b.) stop the granting of additional cash advances unless previous cash advances had been settled/liquidated; c.) send final demand letters to persons previously connected with the Municipality who still have unliquidated cash advances, otherwise, officials responsible for clearing said individuals from money accountability be held liable for the unsettled cash advances; d.) instruct the Municipal Accountant to monitor the grant of cash advances and enforce strictly the immediate liquidation thereof; e.) request for authority to write-off the unliquidated cash advances especially those pertaining to deceased accountable officers; and f.) instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entry on erroneous recording of receivable accounts and ensure that cash advances are recorded using the appropriate receivable account.

We recommended that Management: a) create an Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of all properties with the prescribed report and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor on or before January 31 of each year; b) ensure that all properties of the LGU are provided with tag/s or sticker/s that contain the relevant specification and property number of the asset; c) require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to maintain the Property Card and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card to ensure proper monitoring; d) instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary  adjusting entries on small items and cost of repairs and honoraria paid improperly recorded to PPE account; and e) instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the lapsing schedule showing the depreciation of all depreciable assets of the LGU.

We recommended that Management through the Office of the Municipal Assessor prioritize the updating of its records particularly the Field Appraisal & Assessment Sheet (FAAS) of all real properties in the Municipality so that correct amount of real property taxes may be assessed and/or collected.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:      a) transfer the unutilized fund for CY2018 to Trust Liability-DRRM account in the Trust Fund books; b) prepare subsidiary ledgers as required in Section 5.1.12 and revert back to the General Fund the unutilized amount for CY 2013; c) include all the information needed in the preparation of MDRRMF Utilization Report; and     d) observe proper recording of transactions and submit the detailed distribution list of the distributed 100 sacks of rice to forestall the issuance of Notice of Suspension.

We recommended that Management cause the persons responsible for the acceptance of the bid resulting to the approval of the contract to pay the amount of ₱99,124.81 representing the excess amount of the contract price that exceeded the correct computation of the ABC. We likewise recommend that the BAC engage a Technical Working Group to take on the job of double-checking computations and technical matters relating to item under procurement.

To reiterate our previous years’ audit findings, we recommended that Management: a) stop the practice of resorting to cash advance in the procurement of goods and services required during the conduct of the annual activities/events of the Municipality; and b) require the refund of the ₱36,089.27 taxes that should have been withheld for the procurement of goods and services made through cash advances and remit the same to the BIR.

We recommended and Management agreed to require: a) all collectors to remit daily collections to the Liquidating Officer intact, using the properly filled up RCD and for the Municipal Treasurer to immediately deposit all collections as required under Section 32 of NGAS, Volume I. b) that subsequent collections be deposited intact and daily or the next banking day, pursuant to Section 32 of the NGAS; and c) an updated and complete records of cash accountabilities (eg. cashbook, MRAAF) be maintained to facilitate timely audit and examination of accounts.

We recommended that Management instruct the OIC- Municipal Budget Officer and the OIC- Municipal Planning and Development Officer to coordinate closely in monitoring the implementation of projects funded from the 20% Development Fund to attain the optimal socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes which is the core of the fund allocation. We likewise recommend that Management require the Municipal Engineer to maintain adequate records pertaining to the status of the project being implemented particularly the engineer’s logbook.
We recommended  that Management strictly comply with the requirements of Sentrong Sigla Program of the DOH to fully maximize the benefits that can be derived from the Program.

We recommended and the Person in-charge of the BHS agreed to make > _representation with the Local Officials of LGU Camaligan and  Barangay Tarosanan and discuss with them the problem, issues encountered especially the lack of supplies/medicines prevalent in the BHS.

We recommend that Management require: (a) oblige the Municipal Accountant to maintain an updated SL for its Advances for Payroll and a separate SL for Due from Officers and Employees for all accounts; (b) reconcile the difference between the GL and SL balances ; (c) reconcile the balance of Advances for Payroll account with the cash book at the Treasury for all fund; (d) require the Disbursing Officer to update and reconcile her cashbook with the Accounting record; and (e) assign staff who could prioritize the preparation of updated records of the Accounting Office as basis for the preparation of the Financial Statements.
